title: brain db verify [--backup <backupRef>] — --json success output
description: Read-only integrity verification. Without --backup: runs the store invariant queries from sqlite-data-dictionary.md §invariants (identity-key uniqueness, no dangling FK, active-generation consistency, etc.). With --backup: additionally validates a backup bundle per ledger-backup-contract.md §8 — decryptability, content hash, schema compatibility, and (when a non-local destination is configured) failure-domain reachability. Writes no ledger row (non-persisting; available in the blocked mode). Exit 0 on success, 1 on any invariant or backup-integrity failure.

ok.description: True when every invariant (and, if requested, the backup) verified.

failureDomainOk.description: Present only when a non-local destination is configured.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Verify store invariants; with --backup also verify a backup bundle.

flags.description: Verify the named backup bundle (decryptability, content hash, schema compatibility, failure-domain) instead of / in addition to store invariants.

prohibitedEffects: no ledger row written (available in the backup-unhealthy blocked mode)
no mutation of the store or any backup bundle

errorCodes.when: the SQLite store file is missing or unopenable
one or more store invariants did not hold
--backup names a backup ref that does not exist
the backup fails decryptability or content-hash verification
the backup's stamped schema version is incompatible with the current binary
